target.name: Corintis
target.founder: Remco van Erp, Sam Harrison, Elison Matioli

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Corintis is a Series A semiconductor chip company founded by Remco van Erp, Sam Harrison, and Elison Matioli, having raised $25M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 4 same-stage, same-sector private comparables (Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, Rain AI) alongside 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from the $25M capital raised as a proxy baseline, implying a $50M valuation. Peer-median anchoring across the 4 private comparables yields a $1.5B implied value, with the peer set ranging from $500M (Rain AI) to $5.0B (Etched). Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue figures for Corintis. The public semiconductor sector trades at a 25.3x median P/S multiple, but this method remains inapplicable without revenue data.

The triangulated range spans $42.5M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $775M across the two applicable methods. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Corintis's technology differentiation, IP portfolio, and market traction relative to the peer set. Stage-progression alone appears conservative, while peer-median reflects the recent breadth of frontier-chip funding but risks overstating valuation absent evidence of comparable product maturity or customer adoption.
